name: Loop tour from Manteigas in the Serra da Estrela
name_original: Giro ad anello da Manteigas nella Serra da Estrela
region_intro: Road cycling around Manteigas (São Pedro) offers routes through the mountainous terrain of the Serra da Estrela, Portugal's highest mountain range. The landscape is characterized by significant elevation changes, winding mountain roads, and views of granite formations and valleys. Road cyclists can expect challenging climbs and descents, with routes often traversing through natural parks and past notable peaks. The region provides a diverse environment for road cycling, from high mountain passes to forested sections. Best road cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro) The most popular road cycling route is Loop tour from Manteigas in the Serra da Estrela, a 43.9 miles (70.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through the heart of the Serra da Estrela. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serra da Estrela Loop from Manteigas, a difficult 52.0 miles (83.7 km) path. This route offers extensive mountain scenery and challenging climbs, typical of the region's high-altitude roads. Local road cyclists also love the Cântaro Magro – Serra da Estrela loop from Serra da Estrela, a 19.2 miles (30.8 km) trail leading through the dramatic Cântaro Magro rock formation, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Manteigas (São Pedro) is defined by mountain passes, deep valleys, and granite rock formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels, with more than 60 road cycling routes available. The routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Manteigas (São Pedro)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Manteigas (São Pedro), offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The region is characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Serra da Estrela, Portugal's highest mountain range. You can expect significant elevation changes, winding mountain roads, and views of granite formations and valleys, with challenging climbs and descents.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: While many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling routes around Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Many routes in the region are considered difficult due to significant elevation gain. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2315574642/loop-tour-from-manteigas-in-the-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop tour from Manteigas in the Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.9-mile (70.7 km) route with over 1,900 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2315520069/serra-da-estrela-loop-from-manteigas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Estrela Loop from Manteigas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 52.0 miles (83.7 km) long with nearly 2,000 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find loop road cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32208047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cântaro Magro – Serra da Estrela loop from Serra da Estrela&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 19.2-mile (30.8 km) loop that takes you through dramatic rock formations.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes in this region?
A: Road cycling in Manteigas (São Pedro) is known for significant elevation changes. Difficult routes often feature over 1,900 meters of ascent, while moderate routes can still involve hundreds of meters of climbing, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32206524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cântaro Magro – Serra da Estrela loop from Penhas da Saúde&lt;/a&gt; with over 650 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling around Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several natural monuments and scenic spots. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1258525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre (Serra da Estrela Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, which is the highest point in mainland Portugal. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The mountainous terrain means that spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and less chance of snow or ice on higher passes.

Q: What are the road cycling conditions like in Manteigas (São Pedro) during winter?
A: During winter, especially at higher elevations in the Serra da Estrela, road conditions can be challenging due to snow and ice. Some mountain passes may be closed, and temperatures can drop significantly. It's advisable to check local weather and road reports before planning a winter ride.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with parking available?
A: Many routes in the region start from towns like Manteigas, where parking facilities are typically available. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32149491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Berenxido Bar & Sandwiches – Gouveia loop from Manteigas (Santa Maria)&lt;/a&gt; would likely have parking options near the starting point in Manteigas.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: The road cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro) are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive mountain scenery and challenging climbs typical of the region's high-altitude roads.

Q: Are there less crowded road cycling routes in Manteigas (São Pedro)?
A: While popular routes attract many cyclists, the region offers over 60 routes. Exploring some of the less-trafficked roads, especially those not directly leading to the highest peaks, may provide a more solitary experience. Consulting komoot's route planner can help identify less-frequented paths.
tips:
Cântaro Magro is one of the most iconic and spectacular rock formations in the Serra da Estrela, the highest mountain range in mainland Portugal. 

This imposing granite spire is the result of millions of years of glacial erosion and weathering. Its name, which means "Skinny Pitcher", perfectly describes its slender and elongated silhouette.
The waters of the Rio Mondego originate here and you can find a fountain where you can fill your water bottle with fresh water!

It is considered the "capital of Serra da Estrela" and a privileged starting point to explore the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. 

Manteigas is famous for its wool textile industry, especially for the production of fabrics and blankets. Manteigas is also known for its natural beauty, with glacial valleys (such as Zêzere), waterfalls and unique rock formations, including the famous Cântaro Magro.
